blocks.content: The carriage carrying Armin and his party sped towards the castle gate.
The wheels splashed through knee-deep rainwater, sending water flying outside.
The carriage bounced over the rough mountain path, hitting rocks.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It looks like we'll arrive soon. It's fortunate that it's not far.
Amy stuck her head out the window.
Beyond the sky covered with dark clouds, a massive pillar of light seemed to pierce through.
As they entered that place, the sound of rain disappeared, and a warm, sunlit meadow spread out before them.
Armin and his party got off the carriage and looked back at the path they had taken.
Between the dark clouds and the horizon, almost touching, lightning like static electricity was flashing.
Even in the sunlight, the coachman's face was not visible.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: He must be a nobody, as even Shirone had no particular impression of him.
Shirone was sitting on the meadow below the hill, looking at flowers.
Although his face couldn't be seen as he had his back turned, there was no one else who could exist alone in the realm of the Unbreakable Diamond.
Following a fluttering butterfly, he turned his head, and his profile showed a beautiful smile.
Reina, now understanding the mental world, was amazed.
Even in the horrific reality, Shirone's mind was unwavering.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: She seemed to understand why he could make precise judgments in numerous crises.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: But... should I talk to him?
Reina couldn't easily approach.
Strictly speaking, it wasn't Shirone but his avatar. Since it integrated even intimate emotions, an unexpected response could hurt him.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Let's go. We have nowhere else to retreat.
Armin pushed Reina's back and approached Shirone.
Sensing their presence, Shirone turned his head.
Reina swallowed hard.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh? Mr. Armin? And Sister Reina?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's been a while. How have you been?
Armin scrutinized Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Truly remarkable.
Shirone's avatar was almost indistinguishable from his usual self.
Among the avatars Armin had met, there were famous personalities from around the world. But even they often revealed disappointing natures at the avatar stage.
It wasn't their fault.
That's human nature.
But Shirone was different.
While anyone might deviate when unseen, Shirone's mind had reached a pure state, unaffected by others' gaze.
It was a form of avatar that could only emerge from refining and cultivating one's inner self from childhood.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: The rumor that he's the most promising talent in Tormia wasn't unfounded.
Even after parting a year ago, Armin had consistently heard about Shirone's achievements.
His abnormal, wide-ranging progress, which couldn't be explained by talent alone, was supported by this mental completeness.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: So, what's going on?
The avatar knew this was the mental world. However, it couldn't separate it from the dimension of reality.
It was like not realizing one is dreaming in a dream.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Does Shirone not know why we're here? We've been feeding him keywords all this time.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: It's hard to draw a clear line on what he knows. The avatar governs the whole but isn't the whole itself.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: It's like a core code.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Exactly. While all this is the avatar's doing, it doesn't embody the avatar's will.
Amy's voice was heard.
When the two turned their heads, they saw the Egoist, over 2 meters tall, holding Amy with four arms.
The upper right arm had turned into a rope, strangling her neck.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: There shouldn't be any projectiles?
Reina looked around and realized the coachman had disappeared.
But why did the Egoist manifest? Wasn't Shirone supposed to be friendly to them?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Shirone, what's wrong?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I just... get annoyed when I see Amy.
Armin sighed.
What he feared had happened.
Connecting cause and effect is the realm of reason. Conversely, emotions are true to the moment's feelings.
In reality, the two are strictly separated, but the mental entity, the avatar, couldn't do that.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Shirone, you need to suppress your emotions as much as possible now. You might feel it, but your life is in danger. You need our cooperation. And of course, Amy's cooperation too.
Armin's persuasion didn't make Shirone understand, but he had no choice but to follow since his life was at stake.
Suppressing his emotions, the Egoist became blurry and then turned into black smoke.
Amy, rubbing her neck and coughing, looked at Shirone with sad eyes.
She had been prepared, but being treated like this still hurt.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What's the reason? You have to tell me so I can fix it.
Shirone didn't know the reason either.
Memories related to Amy surged like a fountain, but the avatar, unable to arrange cause and effect in order, could only feel the emotions of those situations.
Shirone distanced himself from her and turned to Armin.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What should we do now?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: First, we need to penetrate deeper into Shirone's mind. If we don't catch the intruder, there's no way to recover the situation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But I don't know the way down.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: No, you do. The reason Shirone can accept the current situation without discomfort is because he knows. It's just that the logical system is broken, so he can't draw conclusions.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Think about going down. Then tell me the first place that comes to mind.
Lost in thought, Shirone pointed to the western mountains.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not exact, but...

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: That's enough. Let's go.
Armin sat in the coachman's seat in place of the disappeared coachman.
They traveled for about an hour in the direction Shirone pointed to and arrived at a small village.
It was also pouring rain there, but as soon as Shirone entered, the weather cleared up as if by magic.
Shirone led them to a shabby stable.
When he hugged a bunch of soft straw on the floor and moved it aside, a door leading underground appeared.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Oh, there really is a door?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Rather than that, it's more accurate to say it was designated. The door exists where Shirone thinks it is.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Remember this sense. At this speed, we can catch up with Arius and Zion.
Hearing the names of the two, Shirone's face twisted fiercely.
Armin flinched and looked back.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Though it had disappeared, he felt a momentary, otherworldly killing intent.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: What was that just now?
Armin realized he needed to revise his thoughts.
Shirone's avatar seemed no different from his usual self, but perhaps that wasn't all there was to it.
Shirone's Mental Stage 8
Arius independently gathered information and delved deeper.
The current place he was exploring was a landfill with a stench so foul it was hard to breathe.
As the unbearable smell wafted on the wind, Zion covered his mouth with a handkerchief.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What on earth is this smell? It's not even the smell of a corpse.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: The fear Shirone felt was manifested as a smell.
It was the scent of death that couldn't exist in reality.
Arius, already accustomed to the stench, grabbed a passing man and asked, "Where is the nearest library?"
The man, covering his nose, pointed in a direction and hurried on his way.
Arius headed to the place the man indicated.
As soon as he entered the library, the stench disappeared, and he felt a sense of relief.
Zion shook his handkerchief and, deciding it was beyond saving, threw it to the ground.
He looked around and said, "Is it a library this time?"
Arius ignored Zion's words and focused on examining the books.
The books were categorized by subject, but the titles on the covers wriggled like worms, making them unreadable.
However, some sections had clear titles.
They were books related to Shirone's major, photonics.
Arius walked along the shelves, glancing at the covers.
Then he picked up a book titled "The God Particle."
When he tapped the wall with the book, a rectangular crack appeared.
The wall dissolved into the air, revealing a staircase leading down.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I've been curious for a while, how do you find these things?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: You trace the flow connected to Shirone's unconscious. Every object has its own inherent meaning.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: It's a field that requires a certain level of study. You need an excellent understanding of psychoanalysis.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: I see.
By the middle of Arius's explanation, Zion had already lost interest.
As always, complicated matters were best left to experts.
He just wanted to extract Ataraxia and get out of there as soon as possible.
When they opened the door to the 7th stage, a plaza unfolded before them.
There was no torrential rain, bone-chilling cold, or stench that could rot one's nose.
Zion adjusted the leather strap of his armament at his waist and mentally prepared himself.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: At least it's livable here.
As soon as Zion finished speaking, a man fell from above.
He landed face-first, his brain spilling out as he died.
Looking around, people were climbing onto rooftops.
There were numerous suicides.
From a window in one building, a woman was seen standing on a chair, preparing to hang herself.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: What the hell is this?
Arius, watching from behind Zion, spoke.
The 7th stage is the gateway just before entering the REM realm.
It's a layer with strong rationality. Projections facing death exhibit symptoms of nervous breakdowns.
Ahhhhh!
Two sisters falling from the 4th floor screamed as if they were being torn apart.
Zion frowned.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Let's move quickly. This place is annoying.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: No. We'll stay for a while. If they meet Shirone's avatar, it will be troublesome. They could reach the deeper layers much faster than us.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Hmm, in that case?
Arius pointed to a castle beyond the city.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: We'll meet the ruler of consciousness here. Shirone's superego. Divers call it the antithesis.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Antithesis?
It's the consciousness that criticizes Shirone.
It's as strong as the Egoist but significantly weakened due to being on the brink of death.
That will buy us some time.
Arius turned north.
They had to be careful with their actions as the nerves of the passersby were so sensitive that even brushing against them could provoke murder.
The place they arrived at was a castle so small it seemed like a miniature compared to reality.
But it had a quaint charm.
Beyond the walls, seven red spires stood, and guards were stationed at the entrance across a moat that was more like a stream.
As they approached the archway, the guards crossed their spears to block the entrance.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Stop. Who are you?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: We're here to meet the ruler of this place.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Lord Ignite is on sick leave. Go back.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: That's precisely why we're here. I have medicine that can cure the illness.
The guards were confused.
They exchanged glances, and one guard went inside.
After a while, he returned and allowed Arius to enter.
Once inside, it was far from luxurious.
It looked like a military camp stationed at the front lines.
Like the citizens, the soldiers were also on edge, but under Ignite's control, they performed their duties without faltering.
At the end of the hall, covered with a red carpet, sat Ignite, resting his chin on his hand.
He was nearly two meters tall, with a stylish brown beard that stood out.
